title: Aldea pinnata
intro:
There is no information on a plant called Aldea pinnata. However, Alangium platanifolium, also known as Alangium pinnatum, is a plant species in the Alangiaceae family.

Alangium pinnatum is a plant with medicinal properties, characterized by its unique leaf shape and woody stem. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Alangium pinnatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Aldea pinnata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to Madagascar and is a member of the genus Allophylus. Further classification of Aldea pinnata is still being researched and studied by botanists.
uses: Aldea pinnata is known to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is used to treat various health issues, including fever and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Traditional healers also use Aldea pinnata to treat digestive problems and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
benefits: Aldea pinnata, also known as the pinnate plant, has been researched for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Aldea pinnata may also have antioxidant properties, which could help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress.
